evidence.quote: the live surface is not a second product or an experiment — it is the MVP product's front end that was never built

observations.why: settles category by fiat, framed as settlement not argument

evidence.quote: We need to define the product value for our target actors

observations.why: frames what facts/definitions must be established, controlling the terms of subsequent work

evidence.quote: We need to clearly unify this with the past mcp only product, these are not two products, they are two surfaces of one system

observations.why: issues a categorical ruling on product identity as settled fact

evidence.quote: Simplify.

observations.why: bare imperative, single word directive with no elaboration

evidence.quote: All notifications and agent messages must be proxies through front.

observations.why: must-modal issuing a binding rule

evidence.quote: Theatre must feel like a chat flow with annotations that if removed would not move the chat bubbles or leave empty space.

observations.why: must-modal setting a design constraint as rule

evidence.quote: Note this perspective read at the consistent prefix (focus is last) needs to cache across multiple replies.

observations.why: needs-to framing as technical requirement/rule

evidence.quote: We need to move commentary, etc to the gutters.

observations.why: need-to directive on design change

evidence.quote: Ask/add note -> always just "send"

observations.why: always-marker issuing a fixed rule for UI behavior

evidence.quote: This is the MVP product, this is the front end that was never built.

observations.why: settles the unification question by declarative fiat

evidence.quote: there is value in the framed primary source material because it is a huge noise reduction. It also saves us a lot of tokens

observations.why: argues one side from cost/noise-reduction evidence

evidence.quote: we're also arguing that the entire value of the product is that it doesn't do summary until read time, right? So then why are we doing all this filter

observations.why: counter-argument exposing internal contradiction via logic

evidence.quote: That AI summary is really a position where we've now dropped at least potentially some of the statements that support it.

observations.why: structural logical inference driving the resolution

evidence.quote: drop all expressions that don't support a position. That's basically the filter that we want.

observations.why: derives the design rule from a logical chain about fidelity

evidence.quote: we need to be in the cents or tens of cents per message

observations.why: supplies the cost constraint as the operative fact shaping the product

evidence.quote: If we move our org's extraction into our subscription keys, that changes our cost profile hugely. Maybe that means we can eat user cost as a loss leader

observations.why: conditional cost-logic argument for a strategic lever

evidence.quote: if you were in the call, you can see the page, and if you click on a node that is linking out beyond the call, you can see it if you have access to that call.

observations.why: declares the access rule as settled policy, closing the open question

evidence.quote: The wireframes page should be built with the styleguide components, and if you need missing components or layouts, they should be added and reviewed in the styleguide first and then reused.

observations.why: should-modal issuing a mandated process discipline

evidence.quote: profiles + flocking + arc as wizard of oz static but interactive page in product skin for demo. v2 roadmapped in product

observations.why: issues a scoping ruling amending prior record

evidence.quote: full depth for now, it's explicit share and this is a demo. only send it to people i want to see full depth

observations.why: settles the depth-of-share question by fiat with rationale attached

evidence.quote: Teams does need to jump the queue, for sure. I'd like to get that done before Fable moves off subscription, so this week.

observations.why: deadline-bound insistence reordering priority

evidence.quote: we definitely want and need to support things spread out over the document.

observations.why: want-and-need framing asserting a design requirement from stated instinct
